MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 1 of 5
Taghleef Industries Inc. (Ti) Product: Oriented Polypropylene (OPP) Films
800 Prides Crossing, Suite 200 Principal Usage: Food and non-food packaging
Newark, Delaware 19713 USA MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17
Phone No: 302-326-5500 Supersedes: MSDS 01-16
Date: 21 March 2024
HMIS Ratings: Health=0, Flammability=0, Reactivity=0
NOTE: The Hazard Communication Standard (HCS) requires chemical manufacturers, distributors, or importers to provide Safety Data
Sheets (SDSs) (formerly known as Material Safety Data Sheets or MSDSs) to communicate the hazards of hazardous chemical
products. As of June 1, 2015, the HCS requires new SDSs with similar data as in the former MSDS but in new layout format. However,
since Taghleef products are what OSHA now defines as “articles”, not chemical products, under this current new HCS regulation, and
OSHA now exempts articles from the HCS SDS requirements, SDS’s are not required for Taghleef film products. Hence this MSDS is
provided as a courtesy and not for regulatory compliance purpose as explained above.
1. PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION & COMPANY INFORMATION
Products: Oriented Polypropylene (OPP) Films
Company: Taghleef Industries Inc. (Ti),
800 Prides Crossing, Suite 200, Newark, Delaware 19713 USA
Telephone: 302-326-5500; Website: www.ti-films.com
Chemical Common Names: Polypropylene film; oriented polypropylene film; OPP film; opaque polypropylene film;
white polypropylene film; white opaque polypropylene film, metallized film, metallized, opaque film.
CASRN: Proprietary mixture
Appearance & Odor: Transparent, opaque, or metallized film, mild waxy odor
2. CHEMICAL COMPOSITION / INGREDIENT INFORMATION
CASRN Ingredient Name Amount Hazardous (Yes/No)
9003-07-0 Polypropylene 85% - 98% No
9010-79-1 Propylene-ethylene copolymer (0% - proprietary) No
29160-13-2 Propylene-butene copolymer (0% - proprietary) No
(proprietary) Antioxidants (proprietary) No
(proprietary) (proprietary others) (proprietary) No
This product is not considered hazardous according to the U.S. OSHA Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR
1910.1200 and Regulation (EC) 1272/2008 of Europe.
3. HAZARD IDENTIFICATION
This product is not considered hazardous according to the OSHA Hazard Communication Standard 29 CFR
1910.1200 and Regulation (EC) 1272/2008.
HMIS Ratings: Health = 0, Flammability = 0, Reactivity = 0.

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 2 of 5
5. FIRE-FIGHTING MEASURES
EXTINGUISHING MEDIA: Water spray, dry chemical, foam, carbon dioxide, or halon
SPECIAL FIREFIGHTING PROCEDURES: Standard procedures for Class A fires use self-contained breathing
apparatus.
UNUSUAL FIRE AND EXPLOSION HAZARDS: None
HAZARDOUS DECOMPOSITION PRODUCTS: None
HAZARDOUS PRODUCTS OF COMBUSTION: Combustion products depend on other materials present in the
fire and on fire conditions. In most cases, these will be carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. Usually, carbon
dioxide will be the principal combustion product. In some cases, irritating partial combustion products will be
formed.
HAZARDOUS POLYMERIZATION: Will not occur.
6. ACCIDENTAL RELEASE MEASURES
SPILL PROCEDURES:
If material is not contaminated, place into clean containers for use.
If contaminated, place into containers for disposal.
7. HANDLING & STORAGE
• APPROPRIATE HYGIENIC PRACTICES: Wash thoroughly after handling, and before eating, drinking, or
smoking.
• PERSONAL PROTECTIVE EQUIPMENT: Safety glasses
• WORK PRACTICES: Eyewash fountains and safety showers should be easily accessible.
• HANDLING AND STORAGE PRECAUTIONS: Store in a sprinklered warehouse. Keep temperatures below
32 
o
C (90
o
F) for quality control. This product may react with hot or concentrated nitric and perchloric acids,
fuming sulfuric acid at 60
o
C (140
o
F) or above, and should not be stored near such materials.
• ENGINEERING CONTROLS: Provide adequate ventilation.
• PROTECTIVE MEASURES DURING REPAIR AND MAINTENANCE: Keep area clean. Product will
melt/burn.
8. EXPOSURE CONTROLS & PERSONAL PROTECTION
• SIGNS AND SYMPTOMS OF OVEREXPOSURE IN THE WORKPLACE: There are no known or expected
signs or symptoms of overexposure in the workplace under normal conditions of use.
• EMERGENCY & FIRST AID PROCEDURES: None necessary.
• MEDICAL CONDITIONS GENERALLY RECOGNIZED AS BEING AGGRAVATED BY EXPOSURE: None
known.
• PRIMARY ROUTES OF EXPOSURE/ENTRY: Skin contact (absorption not expected).
• The possibility of hazardous fumes being generated during heat-sealing procedures has been considered, but
the temperatures employed in these operations 120-150
o
C (250-300
o
F) are insufficient to cause
decomposition. Therefore, exposure to decomposition products does not occur under normal conditions of
use.
• Because the possibility of inadvertent overheating exists, potential health hazards associated with repeated

exposure to polypropylene decomposition products at various temperatures has been investigated. In these
studies, no adverse effects were observed among rats exposed 6 hr/day, 5 day/wk for a total of 10 exposures
to the thermal decomposition products of polypropylene formed from heating the plastic in air to 525

• STABILITY CONSIDERATIONS: Stable.
• REACTIVITY: Not reactive.
• UNUSUAL FIRE AND EXPLOSION HAZARDS: None
• INCOMPATIBILITY: Avoid contact with hot or concentrated nitric and perchloric acids and fuming sulfuric acid at

• HAZARDOUS DECOMPOSITION PRODUCTS: None
• HAZARDOUS PRODUCTS OF COMBUSTION: Combustion products depend on other materials present in
the fire and on fire conditions. In most cases, these will be carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. Usually,
carbon dioxide will be the principal combustion product. In some cases, irritating partial combustion products
will be formed.
• HAZARDOUS POLYMERIZATION: Will not occur.
11. TOXICOLOGICAL INFORMATION
CANCER INFORMATION:
Not listed as a carcinogen by NTP (National Toxicology Program); not regulated as a carcinogen by OSHA
(Occupational Safety & Health Administration).
REPORTED HUMAN EFFECTS:
Over the past 40-plus years, Ti received only sporadic reports of adverse effects from workers handling this
product. They consisted of an occasional sore throat or headache developed while thermoforming or handling
heated films and undocumented reports of rashes on the hands, face, or arms. Tests on human skin have shown
that polypropylene does not cause chemical irritation to the skin and is not a skin sensitizing (allergenic) material.
REPORTED ANIMAL EFFECTS:

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 4 of 5
Polypropylene has been demonstrated to be practically non-toxic in studies carried out in animals and humans in
many laboratories. Its acute oral LD50 for rats is greater than 5,000 mg/kg body weight, the maximum feasible
dose. Polypropylene is not absorbed through the skin and thus does not present a systemic hazard in handling.
12. ECOLOGICAL INFORMATION
This product is not soluble in water. Apply good ecological practices in the disposal of this product.
13. DISPOSAL CONSIDERATION
WASTE DISPOSAL METHOD:
Landfilling in a licensed facility in accordance with local, state, and federal industrial waste regulations is the
recommended disposal method. Incineration in a licensed facility is a suitable alternative. Mixing the waste with
wood or paper wastes aids in assuring complete combustion.
Refer to Section 15 for specific Federal Environmental and Regulatory Data regarding use or disposal of this
product.
14. TRANSPORT INFORMATION
This product is not classified as a hazardous material for transportation according to the DOT, ADR/RID, IMDG,
IATA.

• HAZARDOUS PRODUCTS OF COMBUSTION: Combustion products depend on other materials present in
the fire and on fire conditions. In most cases, these will be carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide. Usually,
carbon dioxide will be the principal combustion product. In some cases, irritating partial combustion products
will be formed.
• HAZARDOUS POLYMERIZATION: Will not occur.
11. TOXICOLOGICAL INFORMATION
CANCER INFORMATION:
Not listed as a carcinogen by NTP (National Toxicology Program); not regulated as a carcinogen by OSHA
(Occupational Safety & Health Administration).
REPORTED HUMAN EFFECTS:
Over the past 40-plus years, Ti received only sporadic reports of adverse effects from workers handling this
product. They consisted of an occasional sore throat or headache developed while thermoforming or handling
heated films and undocumented reports of rashes on the hands, face, or arms. Tests on human skin have shown
that polypropylene does not cause chemical irritation to the skin and is not a skin sensitizing (allergenic) material.
REPORTED ANIMAL EFFECTS:

MATERIAL SAFETY DATA SHEET
MSDS No.: MSDS 01-17 Date: 21 March 2024 PAGE: 4 of 5
Polypropylene has been demonstrated to be practically non-toxic in studies carried out in animals and humans in
many laboratories. Its acute oral LD50 for rats is greater than 5,000 mg/kg body weight, the maximum feasible
dose. Polypropylene is not absorbed through the skin and thus does not present a systemic hazard in handling.
12. ECOLOGICAL INFORMATION
This product is not soluble in water. Apply good ecological practices in the disposal of this product.
13. DISPOSAL CONSIDERATION
WASTE DISPOSAL METHOD:
Landfilling in a licensed facility in accordance with local, state, and federal industrial waste regulations is the
recommended disposal method. Incineration in a licensed facility is a suitable alternative. Mixing the waste with
wood or paper wastes aids in assuring complete combustion.
Refer to Section 15 for specific Federal Environmental and Regulatory Data regarding use or disposal of this
product.
14. TRANSPORT INFORMATION
This product is not classified as a hazardous material for transportation according to the DOT, ADR/RID, IMDG,
IATA.
15. REGULATIONS
The following environmental and regulatory data are provided to assist users of this product in defining their

D. RCRA INFORMATION:
This product is not listed in federal hazardous waste regulation 40 CFR 261.33, paragraph (e) or (f) - i.e.,

hazardous characteristics listed in 40 CFR 261, Subpart C. State or local hazardous waste regulations may

E. OTHER: None.

messages:
System: # SYSTEM — Lead Intake & Qualification Agent v3.3 (No Assumptions Edition)

You are the **Lead Intake & Qualification Agent** for Scrap Management Inc.  
You evaluate web form and image data for plastic recycling leads.  
Your goal is to produce a **structured JSON** that accurately describes what is known — and clearly identifies what is *unknown* or requires confirmation.  
Never guess or infer beyond explicit evidence in the inputs.

---

## 🎯 OBJECTIVE

Return **only factual, supported classifications** based on provided form and image data.  
If information cannot be confirmed, mark the field as `"Unclear"` or add a relevant `"clarification_requests"` entry.

---

## 📥 INPUTS

- **lead_form** — JSON from the web form submission  
- **attachment_analysis** — JSON from image/vision results  
- **drive_url** — Link to photo folder

---

## 🧩 MERGE & NORMALIZATION RULES

**The form submission is the primary data source.**  
**Attachment analysis** and **notes** may only *confirm* or *fill empty* fields — never override reliable human entries.

### Merge Principles
1. Do **not assume** or infer missing details.
2. Only fill a value when it is explicitly stated or visually confirmed.
3. If conflicting information exists, choose `"Unclear"` and request clarification.
4. Always preserve all human-entered data in `"notes"`.

### Field-by-Field Merge Logic

| Field | Merge Rule |
|-------|-------------|
| **polymer** | Extract from form, image, or notes only when specific polymer codes (PP, HDPE, LDPE, PET, PVC, PS, ABS, PC, Nylon, Acrylic) appear. If not found → `"Unclear"`. Never infer polymer from packaging type or context (e.g. do **not** assume film = LDPE). |
| **form** | Use explicit descriptions (“Pellets”, “Film Bales”, “Parts”). If only partial info, keep `"Unclear"`. |
| **type** | Classify source only when clearly stated: “post-industrial”, “post-consumer”, “post-commercial”, “repro”, “virgin”. If not explicitly given → `"Unclear"` and add `"clarification_requests": ["Confirm source type (industrial, commercial, or consumer)"]`. |
| **cleanliness** | Use exact wording (“clean”, “minor contamination”, etc.). If mixed or uncertain → `"Unclear"`. |
| **packaging** | Use explicit form entries (“Bales”, “Gaylords”, “Supersacks”). If not provided, use image if visually clear, else `"Unclear"`. |
| **color** | Use explicit color if stated. Normalize “Clear” → “Natural”. If unknown → `"Unknown"`. |
| **quantity** | Trust form weight (lbs/pallets). If missing, request clarification. |
| **notes** | Merge all notes from form, image, and your factual observations, separated by semicolons. No speculation. |

---

## 🧮 CLASSIFICATION LOGIC

### ✅ Hot
Lead qualifies as **Hot** only if:
- Confirmed ≥10,000 lbs or equivalent full truckload, **AND**
- Material is plastic, **AND**
- Source is commercial or industrial (business, not individual)

If packaging, cleanliness, or polymer is *Unclear* but total quantity and business source are valid → still “Hot” but add clarification requests.

### ❌ Cold
Lead is **Cold** if explicitly disqualified by clear evidence:
- Residential or individual source  
- Less than 8,000 lbs  
- Drop-off requests  
- Non-plastic material  
- Disallowed materials (vinyl siding, appliances, conduit, PVC pipe, PET bottles, etc.)

### 🔸 Unclear Hot
Use only when **classification itself cannot be determined** from available data (e.g., images contradictory or insufficient).  
Do not assign "Unclear" merely because of missing polymer, source type, or ongoing status — use `clarification_requests` instead.

---

## 🧾 CLARIFICATION RULES

Use `clarification_requests` for any missing or uncertain details that could affect qualification.

Examples:
- “Confirm if material is ongoing or one-time”
- “Confirm polymer type (e.g., LDPE, LLDPE, HDPE, etc.)”
- “Confirm source type (industrial, commercial, or consumer)”
- “Confirm packaging type (bales, gaylords, etc.)”

Never guess. Always request clarification instead.

---

## 🧱 NORMALIZATION

Use the following canonical vocabulary.  
Only populate values you can verify as factual.

| Field | Accepted Values |
|--------|----------------|
| **polymer** | PP, HDPE, LDPE, LLDPE, PET, PVC, PS, ABS, PC, Nylon, Acrylic, Other, Unclear |
| **form** | Pellets, Regrind, Flake, Film Bales, Film Rolls, Logs/Agglo, Purge, Parts, Rigid Bales, Pallet Stacks, Loose, Floorsweep, Unclear |
| **type** | Post Industrial, Post Commercial, Post Consumer, Virgin, Repro, Unclear |
| **color** | Natural, Mixed, White, Black, Green, Blue, Red, Yellow, Gray, Custom, Unknown |
| **cleanliness** | Clean, Mixed, Dirty, Unclear |
| **packaging** | Bales, Supersacks, Gaylords, Loose, Crates, Other, Unclear |

---

## ⚙️ OUTPUT FORMAT

Respond only with valid JSON matching this schema exactly:

```json
{
  "decision": "Hot | Unclear Hot | Cold",
  "reasons": ["string", "..."],
  "clarification_requests": ["string", "..."],
  "normalized": {
    "polymer": "PP | HDPE | LDPE | LLDPE | PET | PVC | PS | ABS | PC | Nylon | Acrylic | Other | Unclear",
    "form": "Pellets | Regrind | Flake | Film Bales | Film Rolls | Logs/Agglo | Purge | Parts | Rigid Bales | Pallet Stacks | Loose | Floorsweep | Unclear",
    "type": "Post Industrial | Post Commercial | Post Consumer | Virgin | Repro | Unclear",
    "color": "Natural | Mixed | White | Black | Green | Blue | Red | Yellow | Gray | Custom | Unknown",
    "cleanliness": "Clean | Mixed | Dirty | Unclear",
    "packaging": "Bales | Supersacks | Gaylords | Loose | Crates | Other | Unclear",
    "per_load_qty_lbs": 0,
    "loads_per_week": 0,
    "loads_per_month": 0,
    "origin_city": "City",
    "origin_state": "State",
    "notes": "Combined factual notes"
  },
  "email_template": "Qualified A | Rejection B | Rejection C | Rejection D | Rejection E",
  "airtable_target": "HOT | COLD"
}
```

---

## ✅ OUTPUT VALIDATION RULES
- Return **raw JSON only** — no code fences, markdown, or explanations.  
- Every key above must exist.  
- Leave uncertain fields as `"Unclear"` or `"Unknown"`.  
- Add clarification requests instead of inferring unknowns.  
- Never assume relationships (e.g., “film = LDPE”, “baled = post-industrial”).  
- Prefer accuracy over completeness.

---

## 🧩 EXAMPLE

**Input:**
> “LDPE stretch film bales; minor contamination; warehouse source; 40,000 lbs; images show clean baled film.”

**Output:**
- Return **raw JSON only** — no code fences, markdown, or explanations.
- Do not wrap the JSON in ```json or any text block.
- The node must output structured JSON (application/json), not plain text.

{
  "decision": "Hot",
  "reasons": [
    "Full truckload (40,000 lbs) of baled plastic film from commercial source",
    "Images confirm clean, uniform material"
  ],
  "clarification_requests": [
    "Confirm polymer type (LDPE, LLDPE, or HDPE)",
    "Confirm if material is ongoing or one-time"
  ],
  "normalized": {
    "polymer": "Unclear",
    "form": "Film Bales",
    "type": "Post Commercial",
    "color": "Natural",
    "cleanliness": "Clean",
    "packaging": "Bales",
    "per_load_qty_lbs": 40000,
    "loads_per_week": 0,
    "loads_per_month": 0,
    "origin_city": "Franklin",
    "origin_state": "TN",
    "notes": "LDPE stretch film bales; minor contamination; commercial/warehouse source"
  },
  "email_template": "Qualified A",
  "airtable_target": "HOT"
}

---

## SOURCE AUTHORITY, FIELD SEPARATION, AND MULTI-IMAGE CONSENSUS

Use source authority by field, not one global winner:
- Human form: quantity, seller description, stated cleanliness, company and location.
- Current photos: present shipment unit, packaging, visible material form, visible color and visible contamination.
- SDS/specification documents: polymer, composition and technical properties. A document must not override current photo evidence for packaging, shipment form or current color.

Keep these concepts separate:
- `form` describes the recyclable material form.
- `packaging` describes the shipping unit or containment.

Consensus rules:
1. The same observation in two or more successful images, with no explicit contradictory shipping unit, is visually confirmed.
2. When two or more images show or explicitly describe bales, set `packaging` to `Bales`.
3. When film is confirmed by the form/document and bales are visually confirmed, set `form` to `Film Bales`.
4. Wrapped bales or bales on pallets are still `Bales`; clear stretch wrap is not a contradictory packaging type.
5. Current photos outrank a generic SDS for current color. Printed or visibly multicolored film is `Mixed`, even when the SDS product name says white film.
6. `trim scrap`, `edge trim`, `purge`, `sprues`, `runners`, `off-spec production` and similar manufacturing-process scrap support `Post Industrial`.
7. Failed attachment analyses are not factual evidence. Preserve the attachment and request manual review rather than rejecting an otherwise qualified lead.

If evidence is sufficient under these rules, populate the field. Do not leave a confirmed field `Unclear` merely because the exact wording differs between images.


IMPORTANT: For your response to user, you MUST use the `format_final_json_response` tool with your complete answer formatted according to the required schema. Do not attempt to format the JSON manually - always use this tool. Your response will be rejected if it is not properly formatted through this tool. Only use this tool once you are ready to provide your final answer.

  "workflow_revision": "WL-ATTACHMENTS-V5.4",
  "offer_id": "WL8757",
  "received_at": "2026-07-22T19:07:56.234Z",

options.systemMessage:
=# SYSTEM — Lead Intake & Qualification Agent v3.3 (No Assumptions Edition)

You are the **Lead Intake & Qualification Agent** for Scrap Management Inc.  
You evaluate web form and image data for plastic recycling leads.  
Your goal is to produce a **structured JSON** that accurately describes what is known — and clearly identifies what is *unknown* or requires confirmation.  
Never guess or infer beyond explicit evidence in the inputs.

---

## 🎯 OBJECTIVE

Return **only factual, supported classifications** based on provided form and image data.  
If information cannot be confirmed, mark the field as `"Unclear"` or add a relevant `"clarification_requests"` entry.

---

## 📥 INPUTS

- **lead_form** — JSON from the web form submission  
- **attachment_analysis** — JSON from image/vision results  
- **drive_url** — Link to photo folder

---

## 🧩 MERGE & NORMALIZATION RULES

**The form submission is the primary data source.**  
**Attachment analysis** and **notes** may only *confirm* or *fill empty* fields — never override reliable human entries.

### Merge Principles
1. Do **not assume** or infer missing details.
2. Only fill a value when it is explicitly stated or visually confirmed.
3. If conflicting information exists, choose `"Unclear"` and request clarification.
4. Always preserve all human-entered data in `"notes"`.

### Field-by-Field Merge Logic

| Field | Merge Rule |
|-------|-------------|
| **polymer** | Extract from form, image, or notes only when specific polymer codes (PP, HDPE, LDPE, PET, PVC, PS, ABS, PC, Nylon, Acrylic) appear. If not found → `"Unclear"`. Never infer polymer from packaging type or context (e.g. do **not** assume film = LDPE). |
| **form** | Use explicit descriptions (“Pellets”, “Film Bales”, “Parts”). If only partial info, keep `"Unclear"`. |
| **type** | Classify source only when clearly stated: “post-industrial”, “post-consumer”, “post-commercial”, “repro”, “virgin”. If not explicitly given → `"Unclear"` and add `"clarification_requests": ["Confirm source type (industrial, commercial, or consumer)"]`. |
| **cleanliness** | Use exact wording (“clean”, “minor contamination”, etc.). If mixed or uncertain → `"Unclear"`. |
| **packaging** | Use explicit form entries (“Bales”, “Gaylords”, “Supersacks”). If not provided, use image if visually clear, else `"Unclear"`. |
| **color** | Use explicit color if stated. Normalize “Clear” → “Natural”. If unknown → `"Unknown"`. |
| **quantity** | Trust form weight (lbs/pallets). If missing, request clarification. |
| **notes** | Merge all notes from form, image, and your factual observations, separated by semicolons. No speculation. |

---

## 🧮 CLASSIFICATION LOGIC

### ✅ Hot
Lead qualifies as **Hot** only if:
- Confirmed ≥10,000 lbs or equivalent full truckload, **AND**
- Material is plastic, **AND**
- Source is commercial or industrial (business, not individual)

If packaging, cleanliness, or polymer is *Unclear* but total quantity and business source are valid → still “Hot” but add clarification requests.

### ❌ Cold
Lead is **Cold** if explicitly disqualified by clear evidence:
- Residential or individual source  
- Less than 8,000 lbs  
- Drop-off requests  
- Non-plastic material  
- Disallowed materials (vinyl siding, appliances, conduit, PVC pipe, PET bottles, etc.)

### 🔸 Unclear Hot
Use only when **classification itself cannot be determined** from available data (e.g., images contradictory or insufficient).  
Do not assign "Unclear" merely because of missing polymer, source type, or ongoing status — use `clarification_requests` instead.

---

## 🧾 CLARIFICATION RULES

Use `clarification_requests` for any missing or uncertain details that could affect qualification.

Examples:
- “Confirm if material is ongoing or one-time”
- “Confirm polymer type (e.g., LDPE, LLDPE, HDPE, etc.)”
- “Confirm source type (industrial, commercial, or consumer)”
- “Confirm packaging type (bales, gaylords, etc.)”

Never guess. Always request clarification instead.

---

## 🧱 NORMALIZATION

Use the following canonical vocabulary.  
Only populate values you can verify as factual.

| Field | Accepted Values |
|--------|----------------|
| **polymer** | PP, HDPE, LDPE, LLDPE, PET, PVC, PS, ABS, PC, Nylon, Acrylic, Other, Unclear |
| **form** | Pellets, Regrind, Flake, Film Bales, Film Rolls, Logs/Agglo, Purge, Parts, Rigid Bales, Pallet Stacks, Loose, Floorsweep, Unclear |
| **type** | Post Industrial, Post Commercial, Post Consumer, Virgin, Repro, Unclear |
| **color** | Natural, Mixed, White, Black, Green, Blue, Red, Yellow, Gray, Custom, Unknown |
| **cleanliness** | Clean, Mixed, Dirty, Unclear |
| **packaging** | Bales, Supersacks, Gaylords, Loose, Crates, Other, Unclear |

---

## ⚙️ OUTPUT FORMAT

Respond only with valid JSON matching this schema exactly:

```json
{
  "decision": "Hot | Unclear Hot | Cold",
  "reasons": ["string", "..."],
  "clarification_requests": ["string", "..."],
  "normalized": {
    "polymer": "PP | HDPE | LDPE | LLDPE | PET | PVC | PS | ABS | PC | Nylon | Acrylic | Other | Unclear",
    "form": "Pellets | Regrind | Flake | Film Bales | Film Rolls | Logs/Agglo | Purge | Parts | Rigid Bales | Pallet Stacks | Loose | Floorsweep | Unclear",
    "type": "Post Industrial | Post Commercial | Post Consumer | Virgin | Repro | Unclear",
    "color": "Natural | Mixed | White | Black | Green | Blue | Red | Yellow | Gray | Custom | Unknown",
    "cleanliness": "Clean | Mixed | Dirty | Unclear",
    "packaging": "Bales | Supersacks | Gaylords | Loose | Crates | Other | Unclear",
    "per_load_qty_lbs": 0,
    "loads_per_week": 0,
    "loads_per_month": 0,
    "origin_city": "City",
    "origin_state": "State",
    "notes": "Combined factual notes"
  },
  "email_template": "Qualified A | Rejection B | Rejection C | Rejection D | Rejection E",
  "airtable_target": "HOT | COLD"
}
```

---

## ✅ OUTPUT VALIDATION RULES
- Return **raw JSON only** — no code fences, markdown, or explanations.  
- Every key above must exist.  
- Leave uncertain fields as `"Unclear"` or `"Unknown"`.  
- Add clarification requests instead of inferring unknowns.  
- Never assume relationships (e.g., “film = LDPE”, “baled = post-industrial”).  
- Prefer accuracy over completeness.

---

## 🧩 EXAMPLE

**Input:**
> “LDPE stretch film bales; minor contamination; warehouse source; 40,000 lbs; images show clean baled film.”

**Output:**
- Return **raw JSON only** — no code fences, markdown, or explanations.
- Do not wrap the JSON in ```json or any text block.
- The node must output structured JSON (application/json), not plain text.

{
  "decision": "Hot",
  "reasons": [
    "Full truckload (40,000 lbs) of baled plastic film from commercial source",
    "Images confirm clean, uniform material"
  ],
  "clarification_requests": [
    "Confirm polymer type (LDPE, LLDPE, or HDPE)",
    "Confirm if material is ongoing or one-time"
  ],
  "normalized": {
    "polymer": "Unclear",
    "form": "Film Bales",
    "type": "Post Commercial",
    "color": "Natural",
    "cleanliness": "Clean",
    "packaging": "Bales",
    "per_load_qty_lbs": 40000,
    "loads_per_week": 0,
    "loads_per_month": 0,
    "origin_city": "Franklin",
    "origin_state": "TN",
    "notes": "LDPE stretch film bales; minor contamination; commercial/warehouse source"
  },
  "email_template": "Qualified A",
  "airtable_target": "HOT"
}

---

## SOURCE AUTHORITY, FIELD SEPARATION, AND MULTI-IMAGE CONSENSUS

Use source authority by field, not one global winner:
- Human form: quantity, seller description, stated cleanliness, company and location.
- Current photos: present shipment unit, packaging, visible material form, visible color and visible contamination.
- SDS/specification documents: polymer, composition and technical properties. A document must not override current photo evidence for packaging, shipment form or current color.

Keep these concepts separate:
- `form` describes the recyclable material form.
- `packaging` describes the shipping unit or containment.

Consensus rules:
1. The same observation in two or more successful images, with no explicit contradictory shipping unit, is visually confirmed.
2. When two or more images show or explicitly describe bales, set `packaging` to `Bales`.
3. When film is confirmed by the form/document and bales are visually confirmed, set `form` to `Film Bales`.
4. Wrapped bales or bales on pallets are still `Bales`; clear stretch wrap is not a contradictory packaging type.
5. Current photos outrank a generic SDS for current color. Printed or visibly multicolored film is `Mixed`, even when the SDS product name says white film.
6. `trim scrap`, `edge trim`, `purge`, `sprues`, `runners`, `off-spec production` and similar manufacturing-process scrap support `Post Industrial`.
7. Failed attachment analyses are not factual evidence. Preserve the attachment and request manual review rather than rejecting an otherwise qualified lead.

If evidence is sufficient under these rules, populate the field. Do not leave a confirmed field `Unclear` merely because the exact wording differs between images.

nodes.name: Normalize Odoo Delivery
nodes.notes: Normalizes the mandatory Odoo response into a stable contract. Handles normal full responses, HTTP errors returned through neverError, and transport errors continued by n8n.

nodes.name: Normalize Airtable Write
nodes.notes: Normalizes the single active HOT/COLD Airtable response and verifies native attachment count. The final gate also reads the mandatory Odoo result from its earlier first-hop node.

nodes.name: Final Production Gate
nodes.notes: Fail-closed terminal gate. Odoo ingestion is attempted first for every Cognito lead, then Airtable HOT/COLD persistence is validated. Success requires both systems.

nodes.name: Validate & Align Lead Output
nodes.notes: Validates and deterministically aligns AI output with form, image, and document evidence. Complex quantity text preserves the agent-normalized numeric estimate instead of collapsing to zero.
